items.title: Inside Cardano's 'Van Rossum' hard fork, and what it means for users
items.description: The Van Rossem hard fork moved Cardano to version 11 on Saturday. And for the first time, Cardano's community voted in the upgrade, not the company that built it.

items.title: Exodus to cut 25% of global workforce in payments shift
items.description: This restructuring is part of a strategy to build a full-stack payments platform following the company's acquisitions of Monavate and Baanx.

items.title: AI compute stocks bounce as Hut 8, IREN land billions in new contracts
items.description: The deals sparked a rebound in AI compute stocks after investors questioned demand for new data center capacity.

items.title: Tom Lee's Bitmine slowed ether purchases as it bought back $86 million in stock
items.description: The company added just 7,430 ETH, worth about $14 million, last week as it nears its goal to corner 5% of Ethereum's supply.

items.title: Saylor's Strategy raises cash reserves to $3.2 billion, leaving bitcoin holdings unchanged
items.description: For the second consecutive week, the firm kept bitcoin holdings steady, choosing to use the raised capital to boost cash for paying dividends on its preferred stock.

items.title: Brazil’s securities regulator sets up task force with 60-day deadline for tokenization proposal
items.description: The framework will address critical issues like official ownership records, private key custody, transaction reversibility and system liability.

items.title: A bitcoin 'volmageddon' may be brewing, key indicator suggests
items.description: Your day-ahead look for July 20, 2026

items.title: Bank of Korea prepares for live CBDC transactions with 9 banks in September
items.description: Major banking heavyweights join a massive blockchain network test that will allow retail consumers to trade tokenized won freely across commercial lines.

items.title: Bernstein raises Robinhood price target, cites tokenization and prediction markets
items.description: The brokerage’s blockchain strategy, including tokenized equities and its Arbitrum-based Robinhood Chain, could reduce its reliance on traditional crypto trading, analysts said.

items.title: Bitcoin price hits $65K wall as stocks battle ‘record’ institutional tech sell-off
items.description: Bitcoin faced troublesome resistance at $65,000 while traders still saw a potential BTC price breakout into a “bullish market structure.”

items.title: IREN jumps 16% after raising AI cloud revenue target above $4B
items.description: The Bitcoin miner raised its year-end AI cloud revenue target to more than $4 billion after signing $2.8 billion in new contracts with AI developers.

items.title: Peter Brandt predicts the exact day Bitcoin’s bear market will be over
items.description: Renowned trading analyst Peter Brandt says investing in Bitcoin today, will pay off much better in two to three years than buying AI stocks at current prices.

items.title: Here’s what happened in crypto today
items.description: Need to know what happened in crypto today? Here is the latest news on daily trends and events impacting Bitcoin price, blockchain, DeFi, Web3 and crypto regulation.

items.title: Strategy raises $263.5M through MSTR sales, holds 843,775 Bitcoin
items.description: Strategy boosted its cash reserve to $3.225 billion after selling MSTR shares, while investors weighed the value of its STRC preferred stock.

items.title: Hyperliquid sets 500,000 HYPE stake for permissionless prediction market deployers
items.description: Hyperliquid plans to require developers to stake 500,000 HYPE, worth about $30.4 million, to deploy permissionless prediction markets under HIP-4.

items.title: Vietnam sets fines for unlicensed crypto trading ahead of regulation rollout
items.description: The new rules establish penalties as much as $1,900 for crypto violations, including unlicensed trading and AML breaches, ahead of Vietnam’s regulated crypto market launch.

items.title: Oil prices turn lower after Iran says U.S. talks could be pursued based on national interests
items.description: The U.S. has bombed Iran for nine consecutive nights in retaliation for repeated attacks on oil tankers transiting the Strait of Hormuz.

items.title: AMD launches Helios, its first rack AI system to rival Nvidia, adding Microsoft as newest buyer
items.description: AMD is taking aim at Nvidia with Helios, its first AI rack system, and has landed Microsoft as a new customer, joining Meta, OpenAI and Oracle.

items.title: JetBlue wins Spirit slots at LaGuardia Airport, eyes move to Marine Air Terminal as airlines fight for space
items.description: JetBlue Airways won takeoff and landing slots for up to 12 round-trip flights at LaGuardia Airport.

items.title: Archer stock rips 18% higher as company unveils military craft with Anduril
items.description: Archer and its eVTOL peers are racing for FAA certification to start flying their air taxis commercially.

items.title: What to know about testosterone replacement therapy as the U.S. military plans to test troops
items.description: While some research has shown benefits for patients who qualify, questions remain about the therapy's long-term risks and use among men without a medical need.

items.title: Alphabet stock pops on report it's developing a more efficient AI chip
items.description: The new AI chip, called "Frozen v2," would embed parts of Gemini's architecture directly into the silicon, according to the report.

items.title: Voters wary of government ownership in companies as Trump administration takes equity stakes
items.description: About half of voters believe it isn't appropriate for the U.S. government to own stakes in companies, a new CNBC poll found.

items.title: FIFA emerges as the $9 billion winner as the biggest, brashest World Cup ever draws to a close
items.description: The football governing body presided over the most lucrative sporting event in history and is poised to net over $9 billion in revenue for 2026.

items.transcript_reason:
Could not retrieve a transcript for the video https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=FSt7pOg7G90! This is most likely caused by:

YouTube is blocking requests from your IP. This usually is due to one of the following reasons:
- You have done too many requests and your IP has been blocked by YouTube
- You are doing requests from an IP belonging to a cloud provider (like AWS, Google Cloud Platform, Azure, etc.). Unfortunately, most IPs from cloud providers are blocked by YouTube.

There are two things you can do to work around this:
1. Use proxies to hide your IP address, as explained in the "Working around IP bans" section of the README (https://github.com/jdepoix/youtube-transcript-api?tab=readme-ov-file#working-around-ip-bans-requestblocked-or-ipblocked-exception).
2. (NOT RECOMMENDED) If you authenticate your requests using cookies, you will be able to continue doing requests for a while. However, YouTube will eventually permanently ban the account that you have used to authenticate with! So only do this if you don't mind your account being banned!

If you are sure that the described cause is not responsible for this error and that a transcript should be retrievable, please create an issue at https://github.com/jdepoix/youtube-transcript-api/issues. Please add which version of youtube_transcript_api you are using and provide the information needed to replicate the error. Also make sure that there are no open issues which already describe your problem!

items.title: Sinaloa cartel founder handed $15bn forfeiture order and life sentence
items.description: ‘El Mayo’, who co-founded group alongside ‘El Chapo’, was sentenced to life in prison

items.title: Ebola death toll rises to 930, as outbreak spreads at record pace
items.description: Africa Centres for Disease Control and Prevention and partners plan trial of two existing jabs

items.title: Labour has handed Burnham a blank cheque
items.description: The new premier’s MPs think they have backed a winner but have no idea what his programme will be

items.title: Don’t blame colonialism for Africa’s trade woes
items.description: The continent’s growth is being hemmed in by blocked trade arteries, not arbitrary borders drawn in Berlin in 1885

items.title: US petrol prices climb back above $4 as Iran war intensifies
items.description: Resurgent costs drive frustration with President Donald Trump ahead of midterm elections

items.title: Lockheed seeks European production for cheaper Patriot missile
items.description: US defence group seeking approval from Washington to build out supply chain in region

items.title: The Middle East needs its own Helsinki Act
items.description: Trust is not a prerequisite for peacemaking — and Iran and its neighbours should follow the example of cold war détente

items.title: Andy Burnham’s first speech as PM: what he said and what he meant
items.description: Downing Street address offers succinct outline of new leader’s politics — and the problems he will face

items.title: Trump Administration Sought Phone Records of Times Journalists and Their Relatives
items.description: The government is aggressively seeking the confidential sources of Times reporters who wrote about the president’s new Air Force One. The Times has asked a judge to quash the subpoenas.

items.title: How Google’s A.I. Search Is Imperiling the Open Web
items.description: As Google incorporates more artificial intelligence into search, people are spending more time on Google. Some website operators are crying foul.

items.title: Soaring Egg Prices Are Hitting China Hard
items.description: China consumes more eggs per capita than almost every other country, so a recent spike in costs is touching a nerve.

items.title: American A.I. Giants Like Alphabet Face Fresh Tests
items.description: Rapid advancements in Chinese artificial intelligence models raise more questions about costly technology spending, as Google’s parent prepares to report earnings.

items.title: U.S. Average Gas Price Returns to $4 a Gallon as Iran Crisis Escalates
items.description: A month ago, drivers were enjoying some relief at the pump after the U.S. and Iran signed a deal intended to reopen the Strait of Hormuz. Now, prices are rising again.

items.title: Oil Markets on Edge as Houthi Rebels Threaten Shipping in the Middle East
items.description: The global oil price went above $90 but pulled back on signs of hope for a reduction in tensions.

items.title: Nolan’s ‘The Odyssey’ Brings in $124.5 Million at the Box Office on Opening Weekend
items.description: Christopher Nolan’s nearly three-hour epic was No. 1 over the weekend, with about $124.5 million in ticket sales in the United States and Canada.

items.title: The Case of the 3:30 Emails
items.description: Plus: Are you really paying attention to me on Zoom?
